title: Learning from Home: The New Way of Education
passage:
Learning from Home

Today, many students do not go to a school building every day. Instead, they study from home using the internet. This is called online learning or distance education. Students use a computer, a laptop, or a smartphone to do their schoolwork. They only need a quiet room and a good internet connection to start their daily lessons.

Online learning has many good points. First, students can study at any time. Some students like to study early in the morning, and others like to study late at night. Second, students can study from any place. They can stay in their warm bedroom, sit in a quiet cafe, or even study at a library. They do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car. This saves a lot of travel time and money every week.

However, online learning also has some difficult parts. Students do not see their teachers or classmates in person. Sometimes they feel lonely because they cannot talk or play with their friends during break times. Also, some students find it hard to focus on their work. At home, there are many distractions like television, video games, toys, or loud family members.

To study well online, students need to follow some simple steps. They should make a special study space that is clean and quiet. They must turn off their mobile phones and social media during class hours. It is also important to take short breaks of five to ten minutes to rest their eyes and move around. Online learning is very different from traditional school, but it is a great way to learn new things in the modern world.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students use to study online?

options.D: A television in the living room.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says: 'Students use a computer, a laptop, or a smartphone to do their schoolwork.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why does online learning save money?

options.B: Students do not travel to school.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car. This saves a lot of travel time and money every week.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do some students feel lonely?

options.A: They do not have any family.
options.C: They cannot play with friends during break times.
options.D: They do not like their teachers.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says: 'Sometimes they feel lonely because they cannot talk or play with their friends during break times.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What must students turn off during class hours?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'They must turn off their mobile phones and social media during class hours.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students can study at any time they want.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'First, students can study at any time.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Travelling to school by bus is very expensive.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The text says not travelling 'saves money', but it does not say if travelling by bus is 'very expensive'.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Television and video games can distract students at home.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says: 'At home, there are many distractions like television, video games, toys...'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Most teachers do not like online classes.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The text does not mention how teachers feel about online classes.

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: To study online, students only need a quiet room and a good _________.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'They only need a quiet room and a good internet connection...'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students do not need to _________ to school by bus, train, or car.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: At home, there are many _________ like television and video games.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'At home, there are many distractions like television, video games...'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: It is important to take _________ of five to ten minutes to rest.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'It is also important to take short breaks of five to ten minutes...'

vocabulary.meaning: studying using the internet instead of going to a school building
a system of education where students study at home
things that take your attention away from what you are doing
a standard physical school building where students attend classes in person

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Dạng bài Multiple Choice: Hãy tìm các từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'use', 'save money') rồi dò trong bài đọc để tìm câu chứa từ khóa đó. Đáp án thường giống hệt từ vựng trong bài.

2. Dạng bài True/False/Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi hoàn toàn khớp với bài đọc, chọn TRUE. Nếu thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn, chọn FALSE. Nếu bài đọc không nói đến chi tiết đó (ví dụ: ý kiến của giáo viên), hãy chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Dạng bài Sentence Completion: Đọc kỹ giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS - không quá hai từ). Tìm từ chính xác trong bài đọc để điền vào chỗ trống, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ (giữ nguyên danh từ số nhiều, động từ nguyên mẫu...).
